title: Poliodendron heterophyllum
intro: Poliodendron heterophyllum, commonly known as Mexican cedarwood or Mexican cedar, is a plant species of the family Lauraceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Poliodendron heterophyllum include sesquiterpenes, particularly cedrol and cedrene, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as an essential oil or a tea, and is also used in herbal remedies to treat respiratory issues and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Poliodendron heterophyllum is classified in the family Stemonuraceae. This family is a part of the order Aquifoliales. The species Poliodendron heterophyllum is native to Central and South America. It belongs to the genus Poliodendron. Within the family Stemonuraceae, Poliodendron heterophyllum is one of several species that share similar characteristics.
